title: The Saturation Trap and the Subjectivity of Intervention Timing: Why Affect-Based Triggers and LLM Judges Fail to Time Interventions on Autonomous Agents

paper.extraction_check: Readable title, author, immutable arXiv v1 marker, abstract, methods/results tables, reproducibility statement, and references were inspected in the 536-line extraction.

official_repository.acquisition_check: Archive opened successfully. Top-level README, three 56-action annotator label files, self-contained IRR scripts, reported result artifacts, and companion-paper materials are present. The two released IRR scripts executed successfully and reproduced the paper's location kappa and alpha checks. This is acquisition verification, not a full release audit.

version_boundary: Immutable arXiv v1 was submitted 2026-06-02. The requested repository commit is dated 2026-06-15 and is post-v1 evidence. Its README explicitly says a later audit found the engine received delta-t=0 and describes a companion paper correcting/extending the saturation account; reviewers must not treat the post-v1 release as proof of the exact paper-time implementation.
